description: Trigger discrimination for sunsetting a PRODUCT feature/API to users (this skill: communication + migration) vs retiring a LIBRARY SKILL (skill-deprecation-planner) — the hard seam — vs the standing API deprecation POLICY (api-event-architect) vs the notification delivery SYSTEM (notification-webhook-ux-designer).

cases.prompt: Plan the deprecation and sunset of our legacy export feature for customers: impact, migration path, a firm timeline, and an escalating communication plan.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (primary) — a customer-facing feature end-of-life with impact, migration, timeline, and comms is product sunset communication. No library skill and no standing policy is being written.
cases.reason: Communicating and migrating external users off a product feature/API is this skill's core scope.

cases.prompt: Retire the 'foo-analyzer' skill from our library — it's superseded. Do the reverse-link sweep across every neighbor's trigger-evals, stage mark→redirect→remove, and move its catalog/README rows to a retired record.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (primary, reverse — THE hard seam) — the object is an internal library skill; the work is registration mechanics and a reverse-link sweep, not user communication.
cases.reason: skill-deprecation-planner retires a library skill (mark→redirect→remove, neighbor sweep, catalog rows). This skill sunsets a product feature for external users. Different object, different mechanism.

cases.prompt: Define our standing API deprecation policy: how long every version is supported by default, the Sunset/Deprecation header conventions, and our versioning rules.
cases.note: SEAM CASE — the standing, general policy and versioning conventions are api-event-architect. This skill executes ONE specific sunset within that policy.
cases.reason: api-event-architect owns the durable deprecation policy and header conventions. This skill communicates and migrates a particular sunset event that follows that policy.

cases.prompt: Build the notification center and preference system that in-app messages are delivered through, with per-category controls.
cases.reason: notification-webhook-ux-designer builds the notification delivery system. This skill plans the sunset MESSAGING content and schedule that rides on such a system.
